title: CELP Lab Species Guide
text:
PHOTO CREDIT:https://theoutershores.com/bio/animals-plants-and-algae/marine-algae/green-algae-chlorophyta/
Other images:  
Distinguishing features: Fleshy/spongey to the touch. Green looking in color. Grows synonymous to rocks, can be bumpy or flat. 
Easily confused species and differentiation: UFAC

Extended Description: If you can’t delineate color, use UFAC.

title: CELP Lab Species Guide
text:
Photo from Julius A. Ellrich via WORMS
Other images: ’/images/asfo_2.jpg 
Distinguishing features: Can range in color from yellow/orange to deep purple. Has five thick arms and spiny skin, though some arms may be missing. A bright orange madreporite is the key identifier for this species.  
Easily confused species and differentiation: Asterias rubens (ASRU).

Extended Description: Can be found intertidally or subtidally, and will most commonly be found attached to rocks or other hard substrate. When disturbed, they use their tube feet to cling on to substrate, making removal impossible without the potential of ripping some of their tube feet. 
WORMS page

title: CELP Lab Species Guide
text:
Photo from Julius A. Ellrich via WORMS
Other images: ‘/images/asru_2.jpg’ 
Distinguishing features: Can range in color from yellow/orange to purple. Has five thick arms and spiny skin, though some arms may be missing. A bright white madreporite is the key identifier for this species.  
Easily confused species and differentiation: Asterias forbesi (ASFO).

Extended Description: Can be found intertidally or subtidally, and will most commonly be found attached to rocks or other hard substrate. When disturbed, they use their tube feet to cling on to substrate, making removal impossible without the potential of ripping some of their tube feet. 
WORMS page

title: CELP Lab Species Guide
text:
Photo from IMARES via WORMS
Other images:  
Distinguishing features: Larger and faster growing than other similar species. It has a thick shell and a glossy yellow or brown coloring, and a flat umbo. It is also ypically found subtidally rather than intertidally.  
Easily confused species and differentiation: Mytilus edilus (MYED)

Extended Description: Large, thick, glossy yellow-brown bivalve. Attaches to hard substrate using byssal threads, typically in deeper, colder, saltier waters. They are less suited to the intertidal, particularly the upper intertidal.
WORMS page

title: CELP Lab Species Guide
text:
Photo from Irene Novaczek via WORMS
Other images: 
Distinguishing features: Red algae with fan-like dichotomous branching. Frond is mostly flat. Has a blue iridescence underwater and in some lighting.  
Easily confused species and differentiation: Mastocarpus stellatus (MAST), also known as false irish moss. Chondrus has a flat stipe/thalli, although the edges of the blades might be curly. See here for the definitive guide.

Extended Description: Can be found intertidally or in the shallow subtidal. When reproductive, it will have oval shaped bumps on the edges. 
WORMS page

title: CELP Lab Species Guide
text:
Other images:  
Distinguishing features: Any encrusting (calcified), or hardened, pink/red/purple-ish algae growing in patches. Growing synonymous along rocks/hard surfaces. Can be bumpy, textured, or completely flat. 
Easily confused species and differentiation: NOT erect like Corallina officinalis

Extended Description:

title: CELP Lab Species Guide
text:
Photo from Ignacio Bárbara via WORMS
Other images: 
Distinguishing features: Has channeled fronds. Does NOT have a blue iridescence underwater and in some lighting. When reproductive, can be covered in small cysts/bumps, resembling grape pips.  
Easily confused species and differentiation: Chondrus Crispus (CHCR) Chondrus has a flat stipe/thalli, although the edges of the blades might be curly. Mastocarpus stipes/thalli are curled. See here for the definitive guide. 
Extended Description: Red algae with fan-like dichotomous branching. Fronds are thick and leathery. It is often twisted. Can be found intertidally or in the shallow subtidal. 
WORMS page

title: CELP Lab Species Guide
text:
Photo from Misjel Decleer via WORMS
Other images:  
Distinguishing features: It forms long fronds that hang down, typically from hard substrate such as boulders. The fronds have a unpaured airbladders, with a new one generating each year. 
Easily confused species and differentiation: Closely related to Fucus species.

Extended Description:

title: CELP Lab Species Guide
text:
Other images:  
Distinguishing features: 
Easily confused species and differentiation: Can be confused for Sacchrizia dermatodea, which has a square stipe. Saccharina latissima has a round stipe.

Extended Description:

title: CELP Lab Species Guide
text:
PHOTO CREDIT https://depts.washington.edu/fhl/mb/Hildenbrandia_Ben/habitat.html
Other images:  
Distinguishing features: Encrusting red algae, but not calcified. Fleshy or spongey to the touch. Grows in patches, synonymous with rocks or other hard surfaces. 
Easily confused species and differentiation: Hildenbrandia rubra (HIRU)

Extended Description: Might be HIRU but sometimes the coloring is off in the field. If you’re not 100% certain its HIRU then use URFC. If you cant delineate color at all, resort to UFAC. Sometimes we mess up in the field and switch up the letters in which case UFRC

title: CELP Lab Species Guide
text:
Photo from O. F. Müller via WORMS
Other images: ‘/images/TETE_O.F. Müller_worms.jpg’ 
Distinguishing features: Plate limpet that is the shape of a flat cone and can have checkered, radiating lines. Often has a tortoise shell like appearance.  
Easily confused species and differentiation:

Extended Description: Sea snail that is found attached to hard substrate in both the intertidal and subtidal.
WORMS page

title: CELP Lab Species Guide

Extended Description: 
WORMS page

title: CELP Lab Species Guide
text:
Photo from Misjel Decleer via WORMS
Other images:  
Distinguishing features: Blueish-black shell with rounded umbo. 
Easily confused species and differentiation: Modiolus modiolus (MOMO)

Extended Description: Found in several habitats, including the intertidal and subtidal. Often forms large beds. Attaches to hard substrate using byssal threads.
WORMS page

title: CELP Lab Species Guide
text:
PHOTO CREDIT: https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/183033-Hildenbrandia-rubra

Distinguishing features: Deep red or brown-ish crust growing synonymous to rocks or hard surfaces. Very thin and without surface markings. 
Easily confused species and differentiation: URFC

Extended Description: 
WORMS page

title: CELP Lab Species Guide
text:
PHOTO CREDIT: James Watanabe https://seanet.stanford.edu/red
Other images:  
Distinguishing features: Fleshy/spongey to the touch. Brown looking in color. Grows synonymous to rocks, can be bumpy or flat. 
Easily confused species and differentiation: UFAC, URFC

Extended Description: If you can’t delineate color, resort to UFAC.

title: CELP Lab Species Guide
text:
PHOTO CREDIT: https://limpets.org/rocky-intertidal-monitoring/ri-species/

Distinguishing features: Fleshy/spongey to the touch. Grows synonymous to rocks, can be bumpy or flat.

Please use it for reference as a rough field ID guide, although it is likely incomplete. We will note which species cannot be identified in the field where needed.
